description: Sam Gor is an international crime syndicate based in Asia-Pacific, primarily involved in drug trafficking. It is made up of members from five triads and is headed by Chinese-Canadian Tse Chi Lop, who was arrested in 2021. The group controls an estimated 40-70% of the Asia-Pacific methamphetamine market and earns at least $8 billion per year. It is believed to manufacture meth in the Golden Triangle region of Myanmar.

country_links.evidence_quote: The group is active or working with organized crime partners in a variety of countries, including Cambodia, Laos, Myanmar, Thailand, New Zealand, Australia, Korea, Japan, China, Taiwan and Vietnam.
country_links.context: Sam Gor is active or works with organized crime partners in Australia, and does business with the Comanchero Motorcycle Club and Lebanese mafia there.

country_links.evidence_quote: The group is active or working with organized crime partners in a variety of countries, including Cambodia, Laos, Myanmar, Thailand, New Zealand, Australia, Korea, Japan, China, Taiwan and Vietnam.
country_links.context: Sam Gor is active or works with organized crime partners in Cambodia.

country_links.evidence_quote: In 1988, Tse immigrated to Canada and built the foundations of this syndicate in Toronto. In 1998, Tse was convicted of transporting heroin through Canada into the United States...
country_links.context: Sam Gor's leader built the foundations of the syndicate in Toronto, Canada, and the organization transported heroin through Canada into the United States.
